page: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded text processing. Cryptographic and vector acceleration are supported via instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16), AES, and SHA_NI. Multi-core execution, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached and uncached reads and writes also register in the middle 50% tier.

Given its high single-core performance and compute-focused memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2, the c8a.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, lightweight LLM inference (achieving top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M models), and general compute-intensive applications. It is less optimal for memory-heavy databases or workloads requiring high local disk I/O due to the absence of local storage.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates under the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. Supported CPU instructions include AVX-512 and AES. This profile makes the c8a.2xlarge highly suitable for compute-intensive, single-threaded tasks, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference, offering a balanced, qualitative cost-to-performance ratio for compute-bound workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing and DDR5 memory for single-threaded and compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic instruction set support
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for compute-heavy, single-threaded tasks and small LLM inference
